hawkish_phrases: risks surrounding the euro area growth outlook...remained tilted to the downside, but had become less pronounced
HICP inflation excluding food and energy was at its highest level for four years
wage growth remained resilient and was broad-based
some caution was also expressed about becoming too optimistic
Financial stability-related risks needed to be monitored closely
dovish_phrases: inflation remained considerably below the Governing Council's inflation aim
measures of underlying inflation had remained generally muted
market-based indicators of longer-term inflation expectations...remained at subdued levels
highly accommodative monetary policy stance for a prolonged period of time was seen as necessary
Governing Council needed to reiterate its commitment to stand ready to adjust all of its instruments
summary: The Governing Council held all policy rates unchanged with rates expected to remain at present or lower levels until inflation robustly converges to sufficiently close to but below 2%, maintaining asset purchases at €20 billion monthly, with a dovish bias emphasizing prolonged accommodation despite some stabilization signals.
